description: Phase 11 - Error Messages, Version Format, and Help Text: Polish remaining surface-level differences between the Rust treb-cli and the Go treb CLI by aligning version string format, error message style, and help text consistency for seamless drop-in parity.

userStories.title: Git-Describe Version String in build.rs
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want treb --version and treb version to display a git describe-style version string (e.g., treb v0.1.0-3-gabcdef1) so that version output matches the Go CLI format.
acceptanceCriteria: treb --version outputs 'treb <git-describe-string>' (e.g., treb v0.1.0-3-gabcdef1)
treb version human output first line is 'treb <git-describe-string>'
treb version --json includes '"version": "<git-describe-string>"' field
When no git tags exist, falls back to CARGO_PKG_VERSION so the build never fails
build.rs emits cargo:rerun-if-changed for .git/HEAD and .git/refs/ to rebuild when version changes
cargo check -p treb-cli passes (typecheck)

userStories.title: Custom Clap Error Formatter for Go-Style Error Messages
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want unknown command and unknown flag errors to match the Go CLI format (e.g., Error: unknown command "foo" for "treb") so that the transition from Go to Rust is seamless.
acceptanceCriteria: treb nonexistent prints 'Error: unknown command "nonexistent" for "treb"' to stderr followed by 'Run 'treb --help' for usage.'
treb list --nonexistent-flag prints 'Error: unknown flag: --nonexistent-flag' to stderr followed by 'Run 'treb list --help' for usage.'
Clap typo suggestions (e.g., Did you mean 'list'?) are preserved for other error kinds
--json error output still produces {"error":"..."} with the reformatted message
Exit code remains non-zero for all error cases
Unit tests cover the mapping for InvalidSubcommand, UnknownArgument, and passthrough cases
cargo check -p treb-cli passes (typecheck)

userStories.title: Help Footer and -h vs --help Behavior Audit
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want all help output to include consistent Go-style footer guidance so that I know how to access subcommand help.
acceptanceCriteria: Root treb --help includes 'Use "treb [command] --help" for more information about a command.' footer
Subcommand help for commands with subcommands (gen, fork, config, addressbook) includes a contextual footer guiding users to subcommand help
A code comment in main.rs documents that -h shows summary help and --help shows full help as an intentional Rust CLI convention
cargo check -p treb-cli passes (typecheck)

userStories.title: Refresh All Help Golden Snapshots and Add Error Format Tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all golden snapshots refreshed and new error format integration tests added so that the test suite validates the version, help, and error changes from US-001 through US-003.
acceptanceCriteria: All help_* golden tests pass with UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 and reflect updated footer/version
New error format tests verify treb nonexistent stderr contains 'Error: unknown command "nonexistent" for "treb"'
New error format tests verify treb list --fake-flag stderr contains 'Error: unknown flag: --fake-flag'
Exit codes are non-zero in all error test cases
cargo test -p treb-cli passes (full CLI test suite)
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets passes (lint)
